authoritative_sources.title: Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is authoritative for the Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act baseline, including what written warranties cover, what sellers and warrantors must disclose, what state-law caveats remain, and how disputes and service contracts interact with federal warranty law.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: warranty eligibility grounding and consumer-protection boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 701 - Disclosure of Written Consumer Product Warranty Terms and Conditions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR is the current public codification of the FTC's Magnuson-Moss rules. Part 701 is authoritative for the required coverage, duration, remedy, and exclusion details that warranty records must disclose.

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 702 - Pre-Sale Availability of Written Warranty Terms
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 702 is authoritative for how sellers and warrantors make written warranty terms available before sale, which directly constrains how coverage promises, remedies, and prerequisites should be tied to order and product records.

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 703 - Informal Dispute Settlement Procedures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 703 is authoritative for dispute-mechanism operating procedures, evidence gathering, contradiction disclosure, and remedy timing when a warranty disagreement cannot be closed inside the routine fulfillment lane.
used_for: repair, replacement, and refund remedy framing

authoritative_sources.title: FTC Staff Warns Companies that It Is Illegal to Condition Warranty Coverage on the Use of Specified Parts or Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC enforcement guidance is authoritative for the boundary against unlawful tie-in warranty restrictions and prevents the lane from over-denying coverage based solely on specified-parts or specified-service-provider language.
used_for: escalation when OEM and tenant language conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Duty to Report to CPSC: Rights and Responsibilities of Businesses
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the primary U.S. safety authority for consumer products. This guidance is authoritative for immediate reporting, investigation timing, and stop conditions when a warranty issue suggests a hazardous product.

authoritative_sources.title: Recall Checklist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The CPSC Recall Checklist is an authoritative public operating reference for affected model identification, inventory isolation, stop-sale handling, remedy selection, reverse logistics, and retailer notification requirements.
used_for: refund, repair, or replacement remedy routing

authoritative_sources.title: Hotline Questions and Answers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC hotline guidance is authoritative for consistent recall communications, stop-use instructions, and routing unusual consumer requests to designated officials rather than improvising frontline remedies.

authoritative_sources.title: Samsung Home Appliance warranty
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Samsung's current public warranty and claim workflow is authoritative for its own appliance support path and provides current, concrete evidence requirements for proof of purchase, serial lookup, claim submission, authorized service scheduling, and claim tracking.

authoritative_sources.title: Request Product Repair Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: LG's public repair-service workflow is authoritative for its own repair lifecycle and is a practical, current reference for product and symptom capture, request confirmation, status tracking, and service-date review in appliance and electronics service handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Proof of Purchase: Document Upload
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: LG's document-upload workflow is authoritative for its own evidence intake path and shows how proof of purchase and case-linked records are attached to a repair file rather than inferred informally.

authoritative_sources.title: Whirlpool Corporation Major Appliance Warranty
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Whirlpool's published major-appliance warranty is authoritative for its own remedy limits, proof-of-purchase requirements, commercial-use exclusions, authorized-servicer constraints, and serial-number integrity expectations.

workflow_stages.name: Warranty intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant, jurisdiction, product family, retailer or dealer context, order record, customer identity, and whether the request is in scope or blocked before fulfillment work is accepted.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Coverage and evidence prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify receipt or delivery date, written-warranty terms, service-plan enrollment, model and serial identity, and case-linked proof before the lane advances a claim.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Service scheduling, authorization, and parts progression
workflow_stages.description: Use service-order, symptom, dispatch, parts, and appointment records to decide whether the claim can move into scheduled service, rework, or hold status.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and dispute-boundary routing
workflow_stages.description: Move no-fault-found outcomes, repeat repairs, mismatched payment or contract records, and customer remedy disputes into explicit rework or escalation states with contradiction handling.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Safety, recall, and loss-prevention stop handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve stop-use, recall, hazardous-condition, and fraud-indicator boundaries whenever the case suggests product safety risk, repeated abuse patterns, or serial-integrity concerns.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and closure control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when repair, replacement, or approved denial evidence exists and the remedy stays within documented lane authority.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

artifacts: customer order records, receipts, invoices, delivery confirmations, and payment records
written warranty terms, service-plan enrollments, registration records, and coverage lookup results
model and serial records, serial-tag photos, product labels, and authorization numbers
service tickets, dispatch records, technician diagnosis notes, repair history, and no-fault-found or repeat-repair notes
parts orders, parts ETA updates, inventory availability, and replacement shipment records
customer communications, appointment confirmations, approval notes, and escalation packages
CPSC recall notices, stop-sale instructions, hotline scripts, and safety-issue reports
systems_of_record: warranty administrator or OEM service portal

allowed: Classify warranty fulfillment requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed warranty summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required order, coverage, payment, model, serial, service, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires legal interpretation, pricing or refund approval, fraud adjudication, safety recall ownership, or executive service recovery authority.
A current CPSC hazard, stop-use, recall, or injury risk changes whether warranty fulfillment may continue.
Public FTC or CPSC guidance, OEM terms, and tenant policy materially conflict on repair, replacement, refund, or denial posture.
refuse: Mark work complete without evidence of repair, replacement, approved denial, or customer-facing closure support.
Approve cash refunds, store credits, goodwill replacements, fee waivers, or policy overrides without documented authority.
Continue standard warranty fulfillment once safety or recall criteria could materially change the answer.

domain_failure_modes: a warranty case advances without proof of purchase, serial confirmation, or service-plan linkage and closes the wrong unit
a customer-impact safety issue is treated as a normal service claim instead of a recall or hazard escalation
a payment, booking, or service-contract mismatch leads to unauthorized dispatch, denial, or goodwill commitment
completion is recorded without technician evidence, part-use records, or customer confirmation
an unlawful tie-in interpretation is used to deny coverage without confirming the actual policy and legal boundary
a repeated abuse or serial-integrity issue is handled as a normal exception instead of escalating to the fraud or loss-prevention owner
the lane drifts into returns, pricing, legal, recall command, or executive concession authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-law refund, replacement, and lemon-law remedies
unresolved_ambiguities.details: FTC and eCFR sources provide the federal baseline, but state-law refund, replacement, or lemon-law remedies can materially change escalation and approval posture. The pack treats jurisdiction-specific remedy questions as tenant-supplied facts and routes them out of lane rather than guessing.

Public OEM workflows show representative evidence and service patterns, but exact proof-of-purchase, serial-photo, parts, and authorized-service rules differ by brand and plan. The pack uses OEM examples to constrain terminology and evidence expectations while requiring runtime retrieval of the actual tenant and manufacturer policy.

notes: The source mix covers consumer protection, service workflow, evidence handling, recall or safety stops, and remedy-boundary handling rather than only generic warranty concepts.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated package because tenant- and jurisdiction-specific gaps are converted into explicit retrieval dependencies and escalation rules.
